rulings.Case Title: LEVY HUGHES JR VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, September 7, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 7. Defendant Elementis Chemicals¿ motion for summary judgment is denied. The bulk supplier defense is not available to a supplier of raw asbestos in asbestos exposure cases because asbestos is inherently dangerous and raw asbestos fibers do not change when they become a component part of an asbestos-containing product. (Garza, Jenkins, Arena). =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN DOBROCKE et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: On Calendar for Tuesday, September 7, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 8. Defendant Bayer Cropscience Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternative motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 (negligence), 2 (strict liability) and 4 (loss of consortium) and taken off calendar as to issues 3 (false representation) and 5 (claim for punitive damages) because those claims have been dismissed. Assuming without deciding that defendant satisfied its burden of production, there are triable issues whether Mr. Dobrocke was exposed to an asbestos-containing product (fibrous adhesive) attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s request for a continuance is denied. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARVIN PARKER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: On Calendar for Tuesday, September 7, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 9. Defendant Union Carbide¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 and 2 (sophisticated user defense) and granted as to issues 3 and 4 (false representation and punitive damages). Assuming without deciding that California will adopt a ¿sophisticated intermediary defense,¿ the most likely version of that defense to be adopted by California¿s appellate courts is the ¿Restatement approach¿ that is apparently followed by the majority of the courts to address the issue and is set forth in among other cases in Willis v. Raymark Industries, Inc. (4th Cir. 1990) 905 F.2d 793. (See 74 Virginia Law Review 579.) Defendant has not shown that it has met all the requirements of the ¿Restatement approach.¿ =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BEULAH MAE SIGSBEY et al VS. A.O. SMITH CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Support Of S.B. Decking, Inc'S Ntc Of Mo & Mo To Dismiss Or Stay For Forum Non Conveniens
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, September 7, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. DEFENDANT KELSEY-HAYES COMPANY's Joinder In Support Of S.B. Decking, Inc's motion To Dismiss. The motions to dismiss for forum non conveniens (fnc) and all joinders: thereto are denied and all alternate motions to stay and joinders thereto are granted. This action is stayed pending a determination whether Michigan has jurisdiction over all defendants. While defendants did not show that all defendants are subject to jurisdiction in Michigan, the fnc factors show that California is a seriously inconvenient forum and that, if it can take jurisdiction over all defendants, Michigan is a far more convenient forum. = (220/HEK)
